title: CoreArmorX
description: CoreArmorX is a Paper plugin for vanilla-friendly armor progression, configurable upgrade paths, and netherite armor visual skins with metadata-safe item handling.
body:
# CoreArmorX CoreArmorX is a Paper-only vanilla+ armor progression plugin for Minecraft servers. It adds a controlled armor upgrade path and lets netherite armor use vanilla visual skins while keeping netherite-level behavior. The idea is simple: armor progression should feel intentional without forcing a server to install a database, economy system, custom GUI, or resource pack. This page is a commercial-style feature overview. Exact config keys, defaults, limits, and operational details are documented at https://icewolf23x.github.io/CoreArmorX-website/ ## What CoreArmorX Does * Adds vanilla crafting-based armor upgrades. * Supports visual skins for netherite armor. * Keeps skinned armor behaving like netherite armor. * Preserves important item data such as name, lore, enchantments, durability, repair cost, trims, flags, and compatible custom data. * Lets skins be removed safely with shears. * Supports permission-based progression locks. * Includes admin commands for reload, version, inspect, and item repair. ## How To Use Install the jar on a Paper server, start the server, and let CoreArmorX generate its default files. Players use normal crafting interactions to upgrade armor, apply netherite visual skins, and remove skins with shears. Server staff can use the built-in admin commands to reload the plugin, inspect held items, and repair CoreArmorX metadata when needed. ## Armor Progression CoreArmorX can guide players through a server-controlled armor path instead of letting every tier be crafted directly. Default progression includes leather, copper, chainmail, iron, gold, diamond, netherite, and turtle helmet support. ## Netherite Armor Skins Netherite armor can be visually converted into vanilla-looking armor styles such as leather, copper, chainmail, iron, gold, or diamond while still keeping the intended netherite profile. This gives servers cosmetic variety without replacing the normal Minecraft armor experience. ## Current State CoreArmorX currently: * supports Paper 1.21.11 and above * requires Java 21 or newer * works as a standalone Paper plugin * does not require Vault * does not require PlaceholderAPI * does not require a database * does not require a resource pack ## Summary CoreArmorX is built for servers that want armor progression and netherite visual customization to feel clean, vanilla-friendly, and easy to manage.
